subject: Sociology
title: Online Identity and Performance
description: One-minute skill: Online Identity and Performance.

hook.text: Your Instagram self is taller, happier, and more adventurous than the real you. Everyone's is. That's the point.

buildup.text: Goffman's idea of impression management is amplified online. Social media lets people carefully curate their identity.

discovery.text: Profiles become performances: filtered photos, selected achievements, and edited narratives of the self.

twist.text: The gap between online identity and lived reality creates anxiety, comparison, and feelings of inadequacy for many.

climax.text: Online identity isn't fake — it's a curated performance. The question is what it costs to maintain the show.

punchline.text: Online, everyone is their own publicist.

quiz.question: How does Goffman's impression management apply online?
options: People carefully curate their online identity through selective presentation
